title: Leave a Voicemail
summary: Leave a concise, clear voicemail with your name, reason for calling, and callback number.
learnerPromise: You will be able to leave a voicemail that gives someone everything they need to call you back.
whyItMatters: People often cannot answer their phone, and a clear voicemail helps them respond faster. A messy or missing voicemail can cause delays and frustration.
realLifeUses: Calling a doctor's office to request an appointment
Reaching out to a potential employer after an interview
Contacting a business about a question or order
Leaving a message for a family member who is not picking up
result: A clear voicemail that includes your name, reason for calling, and callback information
youWillLearn: State your name clearly at the start of the message
Explain why you are calling in one or two sentences
Leave a callback number slowly and clearly
Keep the total message under 30 seconds
miniChallenge: Practice leaving a voicemail by recording yourself. Include your name, a reason for calling, and a callback number in under 30 seconds.
steps: Write down your name, reason for calling, and callback number before dialing
Call the number and wait for the beep
Say your name clearly and state why you are calling
Leave your callback number, speaking slowly and repeating it once
End with a brief thank-you and hang up
completionCriteria: Your recorded voicemail is under 30 seconds long
It includes your name, a clear reason for the call, and a callback number
Someone listening could understand it on the first try
commonProblems: Speaking too fast, especially when leaving a phone number
Rambling without a clear reason for the call
Forgetting to leave a callback number
tips: Write a short script on paper before calling
Practice speaking slowly; it feels slow to you but sounds normal to the listener
Repeat your phone number once so the listener can catch it
